name: black-flags
description: Joby Warrick's Black Flags — terrorism history toolkit tracing the rise of ISIS from its origins in Jordanian prisons through Zarqawi to the caliphate, revealing how Western missteps fueled its growth
useCase: Understand the origins of ISIS, Zarqawi's trajectory, the role of the Iraq War, Jordan's counterterrorism approach, and the US intelligence failures
knownRisks: The book was published in 2015 — it does not cover the defeat of the ISIS caliphate in 2017-2019
Warrick is a journalist, not a historian — some analysis may lack academic depth
The book focuses heavily on individuals (Zarqawi, King Abdullah) and may underemphasize structural factors
